$notes: Semantic companion to recipe-lint's grammar check (#496 RC3). recipe-lint reads a driver's `case "$type"` arms to know which step types it ACCEPTS — but accepting a step type is not the same as PRODUCING its effect. The canonical trap: headless `opencode run` accepts a `send` step but stores a leading-slash text as a literal user message, so `send "/undo"` lints in-grammar yet elicits nothing. This manifest declares, per adapter, the step types whose effect the driver genuinely produces (`elicits`), plus whether slash commands must use a dedicated step type rather than send-text (`slash_requires_step_type`). `elicits` is normally a subset of the driver's case arms; a discovery-scaffolded driver (RC2) stubs every standard arm but only lists here the ones it truly elicits, so a recipe needing a stubbed-but-not-elicited primitive is caught as a semantic gap BEFORE a recording is attempted.

claudecode.notes: Full tmux-TUI driver; every dispatched step type is genuinely produced. `send|slash` is one arm, so a slash typed via send reaches the REPL.

pi.notes: tmux-TUI driver. `exit_clean` (Ctrl-D graceful exit → daemon process_exited) + `resume` (relaunch `pi --session <transcript>`, which reopens and APPENDS to the SAME <ts>_<uuid>.jsonl across two process lifetimes so the daemon sees the same session_id row come back) ported from the codex reference for session-resume; one slot, two lifetimes — no new session is minted. `reset_session` (extend-driver, ported from codex's swap_after_slash) sends pi's in-REPL `/new`: pi mints a FRESH <ts>_<uuid2>.jsonl under the same project dir (parentSession:null) while keeping the SAME process alive (no relaunch — distinct from resume). The codex /new supersession shape — the NEW session supersedes the OLD; the old UUID-1 file lingers frozen while the daemon removes the old row via the #169 same-PID cleanup once UUID-2's PID is discovered. It allocates a new slot reusing the same tmux/process and re-arms transcript resolution onto UUID-2, so session.uuids/transcript.paths list BOTH UUIDs and curate captures both rollouts. No `keys` arm — the /tree picker (checkpoint-rewind) needs arrow-key navigation it cannot elicit; that cell is a driver_gap awaiting `keys` via extend-driver.

opencode.notes: Hybrid driver. Headless `opencode run` (send/wait_turn/sleep/start_session/session) ignores in-REPL slash commands — it stores a leading-slash text as a literal user message. The live-TUI path (run_live, #495) genuinely elicits slash/reset_session/interrupt/keys/restart/sigkill/mid_turn_send, but ONLY when the recipe uses those STEP TYPES; a bare send "/cmd" never reaches the REPL, so slash_requires_step_type is true. interrupt (extend-driver) fires a bare Escape at the live TUI mid-turn — opencode writes a step-finish reason=interrupted, which parser.go maps to turn_done — and the run_live router enters the TUI on an interrupt/keys/restart/sigkill/mid_turn_send step just like slash/reset_session. restart/sigkill (extend-driver) kill -9 the live opencode process mid-turn: opencode runs foreground Task subagents as CHILD sessions of the SAME process (session.parent_id), so killing it orphans parent+child and the daemon's transport-agnostic CleanupZombies/deleteWithChildren sweep reaps both (#321); restart then relaunches a fresh TUI in the same cwd. They REQUIRE the long-lived TUI process — a headless `opencode run` self-exits per turn, so there is no parent to orphan a child against. mid_turn_send (extend-driver) types a SECOND message + Enter into the composer WHILE a turn is in flight: opencode's TUI silently QUEUES it (documented default) and runs it as the NEXT turn, and the queued text is not written to SQLite until consumed, so the daemon stays `working` with no flicker; it deliberately skips the turn-detection baseline bump (the queued send opens no turn at submit), and a SUBSEQUENT wait_turn detects the queued turn. It REQUIRES the long-lived TUI composer — a per-turn headless run blocks with no composer to type into. start_session/session stay on the headless path: a SECOND `opencode run` chain in the same cwd is a second independent ses_-keyed arc (the directory is only a liveness gate), and {"session":N} interleaves the two chains. live (extend-driver) is a no-op marker step whose presence forces a PLAIN send/wait_turn recipe onto the long-lived run_live path (one opencode process across all turns) instead of the headless per-run path, eliminating the process-exit-before-poll race that truncates the final settle on long agentic sessions.
